Overview Quick start Schema architecture Interactive samples Root schema agents example extends extensions link presentation purpose rich-text status system-metadata component pattern style theme token accessibility anatomy api content design-specifications document-blocks events guideline interaction motion principle scale state variant aliases category description extends links metadata preview since status summary tags thumbnail
A single sentence stating the primary purpose of this entity — what problem it solves and why it exists. MUST be concrete and distinct from similar entities. Agents use this to quickly determine relevance without parsing the full description. Hard rules governing use of this entity. Each constraint pairs an explicit rule with an enforcement level aligned to RFC 2119. Agents use these to validate decisions without inferring rules from prose.
Min items: 1 Entries resolving common confusion between this entity and similar ones. Each entry names a potentially confusable entity and states the precise distinction. Agents use this to make the correct entity selection without trial and error.
Min items: 1 Common incorrect uses of this entity. Each entry describes what NOT to do and provides the correct alternative. Agents use these to avoid known failure modes.
Min items: 1 Usage examples agents can apply directly. Each example pairs a description of the scenario with code or a structured representation. Prefer minimal, correct examples over exhaustive ones.
Min items: 1 Semantic terms that support retrieval and entity matching. Include synonyms, related concepts, common query terms, and vocabulary that designers or engineers use when searching for this entity but that may not appear in its name or description.
Min items: 1

Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) 0.1 — Draft Specification

GitHub
